rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondent Kilby Stenkamp's Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. Stenkamp seeks responses from Lawrence Porchia, another respondent in petitions pending in this court. If the responding party fails to respond to propounded discovery, all that need be shown in the moving papers is that the document demand was properly served on the party, that the time to respond has expired, and that no response of any kind has been served. (See Leach v. Sup. Ct. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 905-906.) No separate statement is required, and the moving party is not required to show a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the matter informally with opposing counsel. (Code of Civil Procedure, Section 2030.290; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants, (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 411.) Stenkamp has carried the burden in this motion. Porchia filed no opposition. Stenkamp's request for sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.030 and 2023.010(d), for Porchia's misuse of the discovery process in failing to respond to the discovery requests, is GRANTED. Sanctions are awarded to Stenkamp and against Lawrence Porchia in the amount of $480. Porchia to pay to Stenkamp's counsel, Mr. Wilson, the sum of $480 within ten days of service of notice of entry of this order. Respondent is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

Stenkamp seeks responses from Lawrence Porchia, another respondent in petitions pending in this court. If the responding party fails to respond to propounded discovery, all that need be shown in the moving papers is that a set of interrogatories was properly served on the party, that the time to respond has expired, and that no response of any kind has been served. (See Leach v. Sup. Ct. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 905-906.) No separate statement is required, and the moving party is not required to show a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the matter informally with opposing counsel. (Code of Civil Procedure, section 2030.290; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants, (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 411.) Stenkamp has carried the burden in this motion. Porchia filed no opposition. Stenkamp's request for sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.030 and 2023.010(d), for Porchia's misuse of the discovery process in failing to respond to the discovery requests, is GRANTED. Sanctions are awarded to Stenkamp and against Lawrence Porchia in the amount of $480. Porchia to pay to Stenkamp's counsel, Mr. Wilson, the sum of $480 within ten days of service of notice of entry of this order. Respondent is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing.
